response: Japan is a highly developed nation with a population of 125 million people and a GDP of $4.2 trillion, growing at a modest rate of 1.1%, with notably low unemployment at 2.6% and an inflation rate of 3.2%. The country carries a substantial public debt burden of 263% of GDP, though it remains a major global exporter with $756 billion in exports. Japan also boasts one of the world's highest life expectancies at 84.6 years, reflecting its strong healthcare system and high standard of living.
